data.description:
## Problem / Opportunity

Comments are append-only and currently have no effective lifecycle control. A malformed, sensitive, duplicated, or accidentally oversized comment remains in reduced ticket state even after `rebar revert` targets its COMMENT event; REVERT records audit metadata but does not suppress the comment. This can contaminate search results, formatted ticket output, quality checks, exports, Jira reconciliation, and LLM context outside plan review.

Explore a principled comment lifecycle model covering retraction, redaction, deletion, and tombstoning without silently destroying the event-sourced audit trail.

## Security / Integrity Constraints

- Preserve immutable provenance: the original event remains auditable unless an explicitly authorized hard-deletion policy requires physical erasure.
- Distinguish author retraction, privileged redaction, logical deletion, and physical deletion.
- Ensure all projections consistently honor lifecycle state: show/list/search/export/format/LLM/Jira/compaction/snapshots.
- Never expose redacted sensitive content through caches, snapshots, audit pages, bridge payloads, or historical projections.
- Define authorization, reason, timestamp, actor identity, and authorship-signature behavior for every operation.
- Resolve how tombstones interact with concurrent clones, compaction, imported/Jira comments, signatures, and retention/compliance requirements.
- Provide a recovery/undo policy where legally and operationally appropriate.
- Consider size limits or warnings for anomalously large comments without rejecting legitimate session-log content.

## Verified Boundary Finding

Ticket comment data is structurally safe from JSON injection: comment text is placed in an event dictionary and the entire event is serialized through `rebar._store.canonical.canonical_bytes`, which uses stdlib `json.dumps`; JSON fragments are never concatenated. A six-payload hostile-input probe round-tripped quotes, braces, fake event fields, script tags, control characters, shell syntax, and Unicode exactly as one COMMENT body without injecting keys or changing `event_type`. Canonical writer tests also pass.

This guarantee is JSON-specific. It does not make stored text safe for shell interpolation, HTML rendering, or execution as LLM/tool instructions; those consumers require contextual escaping, structured argument transport, and permission enforcement.
data.title: Comment lifecycle controls: retract, redact, delete, and tombstone
